title: Figure 2 from: Ivanov H, Linev A, Pochileev I, Stoyanova V (2026) Droplet digital PCR outperforms qPCR by revealing clinically relevant low-frequency KRAS mutations in colorectal cancer. Pharmacia 73: 1-7. https://doi.org/10.3897/pharmacia.73.e187140
author: Ivanov, Hristo
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 2 ROC curve evaluating the ability of ddPCR-derived VAF values to predict qPCR mutation detection. AUC = 1.00 indicates perfect discrimination. The optimal VAF threshold distinguishing qPCR-positive from qPCR-negative samples was ~18%. The ROC analysis confirms that qPCR fails to detect mutations below its effective sensitivity range, whereas ddPCR reliably detects low-frequency variants down to ~1% VAF.
steps_raw: Figure 2 ROC curve evaluating the ability of ddPCR-derived VAF values to predict qPCR mutation detection. AUC = 1.00 indicates perfect discrimination. The optimal VAF threshold distinguishing qPCR-positive from qPCR-negative samples was ~18%. The ROC analysis confirms that qPCR fails to detect mutations below its effective sensitivity range, whereas ddPCR reliably detects low-frequency variants down to ~1% VAF.
